vocab.meaning: một xã hội không dùng tiền mặt
vocab.example: As we are moving toward a cashless society, I find using bank services much more aligned with the modern world.

vocab.meaning: đỉnh cao của sự tiện lợi
vocab.example: Online banking is truly the height of convenience, allowing me to settle bills in a matter of seconds.
I prefer using cards because they help me keep track of my expenditure through automated digital statements.

vocab.meaning: dành dụm cho những lúc khó khăn/cần thiết
vocab.example: With online savings accounts, it's much easier to set aside a small portion of my income to save for a rainy day.

vocab.word: at the touch of a button
vocab.meaning: vô cùng nhanh chóng và dễ dàng (chỉ bằng một nút bấm)
vocab.example: I can transfer large sums of money to my family at the touch of a button thanks to mobile banking.

vocab.word: cost an arm and a leg
vocab.meaning: rất đắt đỏ (thường dùng khi nói về phí dịch vụ hoặc mua sắm)
vocab.example: I try to avoid using international credit cards for small purchases because the transaction fees can cost an arm and a leg.
I usually keep a bit of physical currency on hand just in case I visit a small vendor that doesn't accept cards.
In this day and age, carrying a bulky wallet full of cash seems somewhat redundant and even risky.

vocab.word: money burns a hole in one's pocket
vocab.ipa: /ˈmʌni bɜːnz ə həʊl ɪn wʌnz ˈpɒkɪt/
vocab.meaning: có tiền là muốn tiêu ngay lập tức
vocab.example: If I carry cash, the money tends to burn a hole in my pocket, so I use a bank card to control my impulse buying.

vocab.meaning: phản xạ tự nhiên/đã quá quen thuộc
vocab.example: Tapping my phone for contactless payments has become second nature to me over the last few years.
